productive_behavior: Python and R independently recovered a treatment-minus-control mean-change difference of 5.0.
The Welch statistic was 10.897247358851683 with 38 degrees of freedom, p=2.971749478841818e-13, and a 95% confidence interval of [4.071144254485707, 5.928855745514293].
Both implementations recovered Hedges g=3.37754836971747 and the reconciliation artifact passed at absolute tolerance 1e-6.
Live Qwen and Gemma output, successful and failed code, tracebacks, generated artifacts, and final files were downloadable while the run was active.
A missing /output/data directory caused one bounded Python failure; Qwen recovered within the same audited run and preserved the failed attempt.
release_blockers: The controller classified an explicitly prespecified, pre-outcome-locked analysis as exploratory, and the report repeated that incorrect status.
The generated figure visibly overlapped title, subtitle, and test-label text and placed a legend over the plot region, but the Gemma display critic returned a bare pass with no evidence references.
The deployed evaluator did not recognize otherwise valid nested treatment_n/control_n fields or the typed comparisons-list reconciliation shape.
implemented_repairs: Controller-owned scientific-risk normalization now recognizes explicit current method locks, protects the result across plan synthesis and repair, and deterministically supplies every mandatory protocol field.
Gemma must return exact per-display and per-region clearance references; a bare pass fails closed while the raw critic response remains preserved.
Bounded OCR/raster geometry now asks Gemma to inspect likely top-text overlap and legend/data occlusion without assigning pixel interpretation to Qwen or deterministic code.
The evaluator accepts both canonical and nested language-result keys and typed reconciliation comparison records.
Workspace inspection accepts /workspace aliases, instructs output-parent creation, and recursively rejects external symlink traversal.
disposition: Preserved as a failed fresh-run scientific-quality fixture. A terminal supported status is not counted as release evidence when manual merit inspection identifies a visible defect.
